title: InvenTree
description: Open-source inventory management and stock-control system for parts, suppliers, builds and orders, with a REST API and barcode support.
installation: InvenTree is a multi-container Django stack. This template runs the PRIMARY web/server image 'inventree/inventree:stable' (gunicorn on :8000), wired to Postgres and Valkey (Redis cache). You MUST also deploy these companion services from the SAME 'inventree/inventree:stable' image: (1) WORKER - the django-q background task processor, started with command ['invoke','worker'] (or the bundled 'worker' entrypoint); it shares the same env vars and data volume. (2) PROXY - a Caddy reverse proxy/static-file server (image 'caddy:2-alpine') that fronts the server and serves /static and /media from the shared data volume; in Kubero the ingress already handles TLS, so the Caddy proxy is optional if you point ingress at this app directly, but static/media files must still be served. The server entrypoint auto-runs DB migrations and collectstatic on boot. ROTATE before going live: INVENTREE_SECRET_KEY (run 'openssl rand -hex 32') and the Postgres password. After first boot, create the admin/superuser via 'invoke superuser' in the server pod (or set INVENTREE_ADMIN_USER / INVENTREE_ADMIN_PASSWORD / INVENTREE_ADMIN_EMAIL env vars for auto-creation), then log in at the URL. INVENTREE_SITE_URL is set to the public URL; keep INVENTREE_ALLOWED_HOSTS / INVENTREE_TRUSTED_ORIGINS in sync if you change the hostname. Email (INVENTREE_EMAIL_*) is optional but needed for notifications and password resets.
